$comment: Compliance banned-pattern registry (CLAUDE.md hard constraint 3, BUILD_SPEC §8). Patterns are case-insensitive JS regexes applied to every line of every text file under src/ (content, pages, components, layouts, lib, styles — see SCAN_DIRS in scripts/lint-claims.mjs). THIS LIST ONLY EVER GROWS — removing or loosening an entry requires the human operator. Keep patterns precise: a false positive costs a review; a false negative costs a compliance violation.
$allowlistComment: Operator-authorized exception (2026-07-20, recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md): the client directed publication of mg-keyed GLP-1 price tiers, and the operator confirmed the override after the full compliance flag (FDA's 2026 warning-letter campaign on compounded-GLP-1 advertising was presented). These EXACT strings — and nothing else — are stripped from a line before the banned categories are applied; the categories themselves are untouched, and a quantity that merely contains one of these strings (e.g. a longer number) still fails. Adding, changing, or removing a string requires the human operator. Reformatted later the same day at the client's direction ('@' → 'vial:'); same five tiers, same prices — a like-for-like swap, not an expansion. Second authorization (2026-07-21, recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md): the two per-unit neuromodulator price strings, published verbatim from the client's product sheet after the constraint-3 flag. The dosing category gained per-unit patterns in the same change, so unit-keyed language stays banned everywhere outside these exact strings. Third authorization (2026-07-21, recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md): the Evolus ranking sentence — the first non-price, superiority-class entry — published on the operator's confirmation that the designation comes from Evolus (also live on the practice's own site); the operator declined the attributed wording after the flag. '#1'/'number one'/'best' remain banned everywhere outside this exact string; the self-test's exactness proof generalized from dosing-only to any-category in the same change (a superiority-class string's near-miss variant trips superiority, not dosing).

categories.name: dosing
categories.description: Doses, units, quantities, reconstitution, frequency/protocols, titration — banned in any form, anywhere (BUILD_SPEC §8.1).

categories.name: disease-claims
categories.description: Treat/cure/prevent/diagnose language and disease names in any context (BUILD_SPEC §8.2, §7.7 glutathione rule). 'treats' is banned; 'treatment' as a noun is fine.

categories.name: superiority
categories.description: Unsubstantiated superiority claims (BUILD_SPEC §8.4). {{EVOLUS_CLAIM}} resolved 2026-07-21: the exact Evolus ranking sentence in allowedStrings is the ONLY permitted '#1' usage.

categories.name: banned-angles
categories.description: Per-line banned angles from BUILD_SPEC §7: GLP-1 weight/blood-sugar/appetite promises, peptide recovery/anti-aging claims, immunity-benefit extensions, investigational-as-approved framing.

categories.name: credential-inflation
categories.description: Amy is an FNP — never imply physician status (BUILD_SPEC §8.8).
